steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Toomer uses figurative language to connect Washington, DC, with the rural South. The correct quotation must (1) use figurative language and (2) bridge both places — not describe just one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "And when the wind is from the South, soil of my homeland falls like a fertile shower upon the lean streets of [Washington, DC].”: A southern wind carries soil of my homeland (the rural South) and it falls like a fertile shower upon DC streets. The simile links both places through a single image. ✓
  • Choice B — "The young trees had not outgrown their [planter] boxes then. V Street [in Washington, DC] was lined with them.": Describes DC alone — no figurative link to the rural South. ✗
  • Choice C — “The Capitol dome looked like a gray ghost ship drifting in from sea.”: Describes DC alone — no figurative link to the rural South. ✗
  • Choice D — “The [train] engines of this valley have a whistle, the echoes of which sound like iterated gasps and sobs. I always think of them as crude music.”: Describes train sounds in a valley — evokes the South but does not connect it to DC. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
